py_libraries.cipher module¶

Module: cipher_module Description: Contains classes for various encoding/decoding methods. Note: The ZigZagCipher class implements a basic transposition cipher using the zigzag method.

class py_libraries.cipher.ICipher[source]¶

Bases: ABC

abstractmethod decrypt(data, key)[source]¶

Déchiffre les données avec la clé fournie.

Return type:

str

Parameters:

  • data (str)

  • key (str)

abstractmethod encrypt(data, key)[source]¶

Chiffre les données avec la clé fournie.

Return type:

str

Parameters:

  • data (str)

  • key (str)

class py_libraries.cipher.ZigZagCipher[source]¶

Bases: ICipher

Implémente le chiffrement par zigzag (transposition en zigzag).

La clé (key) est attendue sous forme d’une chaîne représentant un entier, indiquant le nombre de lignes à utiliser pour le motif en zigzag.

decrypt(data, key)[source]¶

Déchiffre les données avec la clé fournie.

Return type:

str

Parameters:

  • data (str)

  • key (str)

encrypt(data, key)[source]¶

Chiffre les données avec la clé fournie.

Return type:

str

Parameters:

  • data (str)

  • key (str)

str

data (str)

py_libraries.internet module¶

py_libraries.internet.temporaryPublicServer(folder, port=8000, timeout=15)[source]¶

Start a local HTTP server for ‘folder’ and expose it via ngrok. Returns a function to get public URLs for files in that folder.

Parameters:

  • folder (str)

  • port (int)

  • timeout (int)

py_libraries.path module¶

py_libraries.path.batchedFilesRec(path, batch_size=10)[source]¶

Yield files in batches from a directory recursively.

py_libraries.path.filesPathRec(path)[source]¶

Generator that yields file paths recursively.

py_libraries.system module¶

py_libraries.system.countFiles(directory)[source]¶
Parameters:

directory (str)

py_libraries.system.displayFilesRec(path)[source]¶
Parameters:

path (str)

py_libraries.system.filesDifferents(dir1, dir2, same_extension=True)[source]¶
Parameters:

  • dir1 (str | Path)

  • dir2 (str | Path)

  • same_extension (bool)

py_libraries.system.filesToNpy(source, recursive=False, replace=False, extensions='.txt', progress_bar=False, max_workers=0, chunk_size=1)[source]¶

Convert files into NumPy .npy files.

Parameters:

  • source (str | Path | list[str | Path]) –

    Either:

    • A directory path (all matching files inside will be converted).

    • A single file path.

    • A list of file paths.

  • recursive (bool) – If True and source is a directory, search recursively for files.

  • replace (bool) – If True, delete the original text file after conversion.

  • extensions (str | list | tuple) – File extension(s) to look for (e.g. “.txt” or [“.txt”, “.csv”]).

  • progress_bar (bool) – If True, show a progress bar using tqdm.

  • max_workers (int | None)

  • chunk_size (int)

Notes

  • Files are loaded with numpy.loadtxt, so they must contain numeric data.

  • If you have plain-text files (non-numeric), modify the loading logic.

py_libraries.system.inputNoBlocking(buffer=[''])[source]¶

Non-blocking console input that returns a full line once Enter is pressed. Otherwise returns None. Keeps internal buffer between calls (via default arg trick).

py_libraries.system.ipAddressesFromStrNumber(s)[source]¶

Given a string s containing only digits, return all possible valid IP addresses that can be formed by inserting dots into s.

Example 1: Input: s = “25525511135” Output: [“255.255.11.135”,”255.255.111.35”]

Example 2: Input: s = “0000” Output: [“0.0.0.0”]

Example 3: Input: s = “101023” Output: [“1.0.10.23”,”1.0.102.3”,”10.1.0.23”,”10.10.2.3”,”101.0.2.3”]

Return type:

list[str]

Parameters:

s (str)

py_libraries.system.unicDirsRec(path)[source]¶
Parameters:

path (str)

py_libraries.temp module¶

py_libraries.temp.scheduleTomorrowExecution(period, distribution='gaussian', possibilities=None, verbose=0)[source]¶

Schedule execution for tomorrow within a specified time period.

Parameters:

  • period (str | list[float]) –

    • If str: must be a key in possibilities (e.g., “morning”, “night”).

    • If list: must contain exactly two floats/ints [hour_start, hour_end].

  • distribution (str) –

    • “linear” : uniform random hour between start and end.

    • ”gaussian”/”normal” : Gaussian random hour within range (via getRandomGaussianWithBorn).

  • possibilities (dict | None) –

    Mapping of named periods to [hour_start, hour_end]. If None, defaults to:

    {

    “morning” : [6, 12], “noon” : [12, 14], “afternoon” : [12, 18], “evening” : [18, 23], “night” : [1, 6], “day” : [6, 18], “all” : [6, 23],

    }

  • verbose (int) – If >0, prints the chosen execution time.

Raises:

  • ValueError – If period is invalid or distribution is unsupported.

  • TypeError – If period is not str or list.

  • Behavior –

  • -------- –

  • - Chooses a random hour in the requested period (linear or gaussian). –

  • - Schedules execution time for tomorrow. –

  • - Sleeps until that time is reached. –

py_libraries.temp.wait(lower=0.5, upper=3.0)[source]¶

Return a randomized delay sampled from a truncated normal distribution.

Parameters:

  • lower (float) – Minimum wait time in seconds (default 0.5).

  • upper (float) – Maximum wait time in seconds (default 3.0).

Returns:

Randomized delay in seconds, truncated within [lower, upper].

Return type:

float

class Color[source]¶

Bases: object

static all()[source]¶

All named colors from matplotlib (base, CSS4, Tableau, XKCD).

static base()[source]¶

Base colors (short names).

static complementary(color_name)[source]¶

Return hex code for the complementary color of a named color.

static css4()[source]¶

CSS4 named colors.

static dark(threshold=0.5)[source]¶

Named colors with luminance at or below threshold (dark colors).

static darken(color_name, factor=0.2)[source]¶

Darken a named color by a given factor (0..1).

static grayscale(tolerance=1e-06)[source]¶

Named colors where R≈G≈B within tolerance.

static hex_values()[source]¶

All hex values of named colors.

static light()[source]¶

Named colors whose names start with ‘light’ (case-insensitive).

static lighten(color_name, factor=0.2)[source]¶

Lighten a named color by a given factor (0..1).

static luminance(color_name)[source]¶

Return relative luminance [0..1] of a named color.

static other()[source]¶

Colors not in base, tableau, CSS4, or XKCD.

static primary(dominance=0.1)[source]¶

Colors where one channel exceeds the other two by ‘dominance’.

static random(n=5, seed=None)[source]¶

Random subset of n named colors.

static sorted_by_hue(include_hex=False)[source]¶

Return colors sorted by HSV hue. Optionally include hex codes.

static tableau()[source]¶

Tableau colors (named palette colors).

static verify()[source]¶

Verify that named mappings are consistent and unique.

static xkcd()[source]¶

XKCD survey colors (prefixed names).

class Plot[source]¶

Bases: object

Unified plotting interface. All methods accept: - *args: each can be

  • y-array

  • (x, y)

  • (x, y, {opts})

  • fig, ax: pass existing fig/ax if desired.

  • path (opt), show (bool)

  • global styling kwargs: title, xlabel, ylabel, grid, xlim, ylim, legend, xscale, yscale

static candles(data, *, width=0.6, open_color='green', close_color='red', wick_width=1.0, legend=True, **kwargs)[source]¶

OHLC candlestick chart. data is an (N,4) array of [open, high, low, close].

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

Parameters:

  • data (ndarray)

  • width (float)

  • open_color (str)

  • close_color (str)

  • wick_width (float)

  • legend (bool)

static errorbar(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Scatter / dot plot.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

static heatmap(*args, bins=100, cmap='viridis', **kwargs)[source]¶

2D density heatmap.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

Parameters:

  • bins (int)

  • cmap (str)

static histogram(data, bins=30, **kwargs)[source]¶

Univariate histogram.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

Parameters:

  • data (List | ndarray)

  • bins (int)

static plot(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Simple line plot.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

static scatter(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Scatter / dot plot.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

static scatter_density(*args, alpha=False, rate=False, bw_method=None, cbar_label=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Scatter plot colored by a 2D Gaussian KDE estimate of density.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

Parameters:

  • bw_method (str | float | None)

  • cbar_label (str | None)

Parameters: - *args: each of

  • y-array

  • (x, y)

  • (x, y, {opts})

  • bw_method: passed to gaussian_kde (None for scott’s rule, or float/string)

  • cbar_label: label for the density colorbar. If none no color bar

  • plus all the usual kwargs (fig, ax, title, xlabel, path, show, etc.)

static save(fig, file_path, parents=True, exist_ok=True)[source]¶
py_libraries.visualize_deprecate.candles(candles, *args, x_offset=0, alpha=1.0, open_color='green', close_color='red', open_legend='', close_legend='', body_width=0.6, wick_linewidth=1.0, **kwargs)[source]¶
py_libraries.visualize_deprecate.curves(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Plots one or more curves. Supports coloring with a colormap (c) and plotting on a provided figure/axes.
